summary: Package an alpha release candidate and make a disciplined evidence-based decision about the next expansion.

lane_1_scout_refinement.selected_scope: Add a strict alpha RC wrapper command around the existing review-drop pack and clean-extract verifier once packet 156 is fully clear.
main_contract: Add npm run alpha:rc backed by scripts/create-alpha-rc.mjs.
Run source preflight before packaging: npm run validate:agents, npm run science:check, npm test, and npm run build.
After preflight passes, run npm run review:pack, capture the archive path, then run npm run review:verify -- <archive>.
Print the verified archive path at the end and leave generated output under ignored output/review-drops/.
Update README and docs/review-drop-checklist.md so reviewers know the RC command and the manual fallback.
blockers: Known lane-2 High Pass rime-footing exact-copy failure in npm test
non_goals: No authored science copy, biome geometry, route prose, station UI, save schema, or map behavior changes.
Do not relax review:verify or commit generated review archives / .tmp extracts.
acceptance: npm run validate:agents, npm run science:check, npm test, npm run build, npm run alpha:rc, and git diff --check pass when blockers are clear.

lane_4_scout_refinement.selected_scope: Create a final lane-4 route/support RC signoff report using existing full-arc proof and focused route smoke, without changing route behavior.
evidence: The alpha route playthrough checklist already maps the reviewer-facing route spine from Shore Shelter through filed High Pass.
The full-arc route-state matrix already covers all 11 live Route v2 notebook routes through active, clue progress, ready-to-file, filing, and next-state handoff.
Runtime smoke already files High Pass from the live talus-hold loop and verifies the filed notice plus cleared active request, route marker/replay label, and journal request.
Packet 156 lane 4 just closed the remaining Shore/Open ready-to-file marker and replay suppression proof.
main_contract: Add a lane-4 signoff report that cites the checklist, full-arc route-state matrix, High Pass live filing smoke, and packet 156 ready-to-file suppression guards.
Record the focused route/state/runtime verification commands and results.
Include a narrow post-alpha route/support opportunity list, explicitly deferred until after the RC.
Do not change route runtime, route definitions, station UI, world-map behavior, save schema, support UI, science copy, geometry, review packaging, or alpha:rc scripts.
Promote ECO-20260420-critic-437 only if the focused route/support checks pass.
non_goals: No new route behavior, new route framework, new station page, new HUD, broad copy rewrite, lane-2 High Pass copy change, lane-3 screenshot work, packaging script, generated archive, or full npm test gate.
Full npm test and alpha:rc remain lane-1 RC gates after cross-lane blockers clear.
recommended_checks: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "Route v2|High Pass|Open To Shelter|Shore Shelter|route-marker|replay"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "High Pass|Open To Shelter|Shore Shelter|Route|routes page|filed"

lane_4_main_result.summary: Added the final lane-4 route/support RC signoff report without changing route behavior.
behavior_notes: The signoff cites the alpha route playthrough checklist, full-arc route-state matrix, live High Pass filing smoke, and packet 156 ready-to-file suppression guards.
The report records focused route-state, full field-request matrix, and focused runtime route-smoke verification results.
Post-alpha route/support opportunities are deferred until after the RC and stay within existing route/support seams.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "Route v2|High Pass|Open To Shelter|Shore Shelter|route-marker|replay"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "High Pass|Open To Shelter|Shore Shelter|Route|routes page|filed"

lane_4_critic_result.summary: Reviewed the final lane-4 route/support RC signoff cleanly.
behavior_notes: The review found the signoff accurately cites existing route proof without adding behavior.
The post-alpha route/support opportunity list remains deferred and uses existing route/support seams.
Full npm test and alpha:rc remain lane-1 RC gates after cross-lane blockers clear.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "Route v2|High Pass|Open To Shelter|Shore Shelter|route-marker|replay"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "High Pass|Open To Shelter|Shore Shelter|Route|routes page|filed"

lane_3_scout_refinement.selected_scope: Create the final lane-3 alpha spatial contact-sheet/signoff report using the existing manifest and recent ignored browser proofs; add a short deferred post-alpha spatial opportunity list.
evidence: docs/alpha-screenshot-proof-manifest.md already names the representative five-biome contact-sheet recipe and capture rules.
The manifest quick-path check currently finds all listed existing reference screenshots/state pairs.
Packet 154 lane-3 representative browser proof cleared with 20 passing assertions and empty console errors.
Packet 155 and 156 lane-3 feedback proofs cleared the first-session beach objective and Coastal Scrub Open To Shelter opener.
No geometry or runtime source change is needed for the RC signoff unless a required local artifact is missing during main.
main_contract: Create a lane-3 RC signoff report that maps final spatial rows to existing or freshly assembled ignored screenshot artifacts.
Use the manifest's five representative rows: beach/coast, Coastal Scrub handoff, forest/Root Hollow, Treeline/High Pass, and tundra relief.

If producing a visual contact sheet, keep it under ignored output/lane-3-main-436-alpha-contact-sheet/ and build it with a one-off output-local HTML/Playwright or browser composition rather than adding source dependencies.
Record whether each row is fresh proof, current reference proof, or older manifest reference, and do not promote older references to hard baselines when paired state is missing.
Add a compact post-alpha spatial opportunity list deferred until after RC.
Promote ECO-20260420-critic-436 only if the signoff report is clear and metadata validation passes.
post_alpha_opportunities: Fresh recapture all manifest frames after RC if reviewers need a visual bundle.
Add exact Root Hollow cave/trunk and upper-return debug save snapshots if repeated visual recapture stays brittle.
Add lightweight contact-sheet tooling only if reviewers repeatedly need regenerated visual bundles.
Deepen current five-biome spatial memory before opening a new biome, with forest cave/canopy and high-country relief as the strongest lane-3 candidates.
non_goals: No runtime/gameplay behavior changes unless a required proof artifact is missing and a tiny local capture helper is unavoidable.

No new geometry, route objectives, station UI, route/support behavior, save schema, science copy, journal copy, new biome, screenshot baseline system, committed generated images, npm dependency, or alpha:rc gate work.
Do not turn older manifest references into hard regression baselines without fresh paired state.

lane_3_main_result.summary: Added the final lane-3 alpha spatial RC contact-sheet/signoff report and generated ignored local contact-sheet artifacts without runtime or geometry changes.
behavior_notes: The contact sheet mixes fresh beach/Coastal Scrub proof with older manifest references for forest, High Pass, and tundra.
Older manifest references are explicitly review aids, not hard baselines without future fresh paired state.
The post-alpha spatial opportunity list is deferred until after RC and favors fresh recapture, exact Root Hollow snapshots, optional tooling, and deeper current-biome spatial memory.
verification: source-index generation preflight confirmed every referenced source image/state exists locally
lane_3_main_result.runtime_changes: None; this was a report/contact-sheet-only RC signoff.

lane_3_critic_result.summary: Reviewed the final lane-3 alpha spatial RC contact-sheet/signoff cleanly.
notes: The signoff report maps the final spatial rows to ignored contact-sheet artifacts and source paths.
The visual contact sheet covers beach, Coastal Scrub, forest / Root Hollow, Treeline / High Pass, and tundra relief.
Older manifest references remain review aids, not hard baselines without future fresh paired state.
No runtime source, geometry, route state, station UI, save schema, science copy, journal copy, new biome work, screenshot baseline system, new dependency, or lane-1 RC packaging drifted into lane 3.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Alpha RC is source-complete and documented; post-alpha scope gate says continue hardening, deepen current five biomes, or begin a scoped next chapter.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Own RC packaging, command verification, README/progress sync, and source-complete archive instructions.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Own final alpha content/science signoff and post-alpha content risk list.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Own final screenshot/contact-sheet proof and post-alpha spatial opportunity list.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Own final full-arc route smoke and post-alpha route/support opportunity list.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
